(4 comments)

Patch Set 2:

(3 comments)

It's good to make these more resilient, but it would be nice to get more debug, since I think Aurelien is able to reproduce (?)

In fact, we might just give this patch to Aurelien and it will blow up a few calls further on rather than here, so we should probably get whatever debug we can, since these situations are supposed to be impossible?

Patch Set 3:

I posted a detailed crash analysis for that in the original ticket. 

It looks like the problem happening here is that obd_device was freed and is still use.
As this is happening during error scenario, maybe that obd_device ref was added somewhere even if it was not really initialized.

parents.subject: LU-16232 script: fix the argument parse

commit.subject: LU-16816 obdclass: make import_event more robust
commit.message:
LU-16816 obdclass: make import_event more robust

Make mdc_import_event and osc_import_event more robust, by not
assuming input variables can be dereferenced.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I31a6477d58b7bb9a557ea561f7b0fa3fbcae5762
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/50915
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
